rulings.Case Title: ALDINO SANI VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 7, 2008, line 8. DEFENDANT SLAKEY BROTHERS, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Defendant shifts the burden of producing evidence. The declarations of Sani and Ay create a triable issue of fact. The Sani declaration is not subject to a motion to strike under D'Amico and Ay is qualified to give the opinion stated. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: PAULINE GAW VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 7, 2008, line 1. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Plaintiff has not plead a claim based on negligent driving. Defendant shifted the burden of producing evidence based on the declarations and deposition testimony on the dangerous condition claim. Plaintiff's evidence does not create a triable issue of fact for the evidence is directed to negligent driving, not a dangerous condition of public property. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: STUDIO MEHLER, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. QUAKERMAID CABINETS, INC., A PENNSYLVANIA et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 7, 2008, line 2. CROSS DEFENDANTS DORIS MEHLER, STUDIO MEHLER, JAMES SAYLES AND KEN BUGHARDT'S DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is overruled. Cross-defendants to answer within 10 fays. Although the partnership claims do not appear strong, the facts are not sufficiently clear to sustain the demurrer. The merits of the claim may be readily tested by summary adjudication. The corporate claims are merely an alternative pleading. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: DEANNA L AUSSELET et al VS. WPX COURIER.LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-08-481125 WITH CASE CGC-07-468686
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 7, 2008, line 10. DEFENDANTS WPX COURIER.LLC AND RAPID DELIVERY SOLUTIONS, LLC'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-08-481125 WITH CASE CGC-07-468686 is off calendar at the request of the moving party. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: ERNESTO ESTENOZ VS. FRIST AMERICAN TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 7, 2008, line 3. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied because plaintiff is seeking and is entitled to maintain an action to quiet title and declaratory relief based on defendant's alleged failure to comply with Government Code section 2728.5. The reference to the subdivision map in the deed is not sufficient compliance with section 2728.5. 1119 Delaware v. Continental Land Title Co. (1993) 16 Cal. App. 4th 992. Addressing defendant's motion for summary adjudication, the Court grants the motion as to the first cause of action for negligence, fifth cause of action for breach of warranty and sixth cause of action breach of contract for failure of plaintiff to comply with Government Code 900 et seq. As to the remaining claims, there is a triable issue of fact as to when plaintiff had notice of the restrictions on the property. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD G. HORNBY VS. CHINA BASIN BALLPARK COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 7, 2008, line 4. PLAINTIFF RICHARD HORNBY'S Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint is granted. Defendant has not demonstrated prejudice. There is time for defendant to challenge the amendment either by demurrer or summary adjudication before the current trial date. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: ERIC KASTELL et al VS. SALVADOR CHAVEZ et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 7, 2008, line 5. DEFENDANT SALVADOR CHAVEZ'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is granted. No opposition filed. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: ERIC KASTELL et al VS. SALVADOR CHAVEZ et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 7, 2008, line 5. DEFENDANT SALVADOR CHAVEZ"S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. No opposition filed. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: MYDRA MCGARR VS. TENDERLOIN HOUSING CLINIC, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 7, 2008, line 6. DEFENDANT TENDERLOIN HOUSING CLINIC, INC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled, 10 days to answer. The facts alleged state claims not barred by workers compensation. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: ALEXANDER ANOLIK VS. THE REITMAN GROUP, A PARTNERSHIP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Change In Order To Consolidate With Pending Santa Clara County Superior Court Action, Request For Costs And Attorneys' Fees
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 7, 2008, line 7. DEFENDANTS THE REITMAN GROUP AND KURT REITMAN'S Motion To Change In Order To Consolidate With Pending Santa Clara County Superior Court Action, Request For Costs And Attorneys' Fees is granted. This is a mixed action case and venue is proper where defendant resides. Moreover, plaintiff and defendant agreed to venue the contract cause of action in Santa Clara County. Given the terms of the parties' agreement, and the applicable law, defendant is entitled to reasonable costs and attorneys' fees in bringing this motion. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: MARK WILLIAMS VS. JOSE LUIS HERNANDEZ et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 7, 2008, line 9. PLAINTIFF MARK WILLIAMS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. A hearing is required. =(302/PJM/VC)
